definitions.text: A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.
To change the size of something whilst maintaining proportion; especially to change a process in order to produce much larger amounts of the final product.
examples: We should scale that up by a factor of 10.

definitions.text: To climb to the top of.
examples: Hilary and Norgay were the first known to have scaled Everest.

definitions.text: Part of an overlapping arrangement of many small, flat and hard pieces of keratin covering the skin of an animal, particularly a fish or reptile.
A small piece of pigmented chitin, many of which coat the wings of a butterfly or moth to give them their color.

examples: Please scale that fish for dinner.

definitions.text: To become scaly; to produce or develop scales.
examples: The dry weather is making my skin scale.

definitions.text: To strip or clear of scale; to descale.
examples: to scale the inside of a boiler

definitions.text: A device to measure mass or weight.
examples: After the long, lazy winter I was afraid to get on the scale.
